module_info.docstring:
Data Flow Graph (DFG) extraction for multi-language code analysis.

Provides DFG extraction for:
- Python (via ast module)
- TypeScript/JavaScript (via tree-sitter)
- Go (via tree-sitter)
- Rust (via tree-sitter)

Based on pflux/python-program-analysis architecture:
- DEFINITION: variable assignments (x = ...)
- UPDATE: in-place modifications (x += ..., x.append())
- USE: variable reads

Uses reaching definitions analysis on CFG to build def-use chains.

classes.name: VarRef
classes.docstring:
A reference to a variable (definition or use).

Reference types:
- "definition": variable assignment (x = ...)
- "update": in-place modification (x += ..., x.append())
- "use": variable read

classes.name: DataflowEdge
classes.docstring:
A def-use relationship connecting a definition to a use.

Represents that the value defined at def_ref may flow to use_ref.

classes.name: DFGInfo
classes.docstring:
Data flow graph for a function.

Provides:
- All variable references (definitions and uses)
- Def-use chains (dataflow edges)
- Variable grouping for quick lookup

methods.name: variables
methods.docstring: Group references by variable name.

classes.name: PythonDefUseVisitor
classes.docstring:
Extract variable definitions and uses from Python AST.

Builds a list of VarRefs for all variable references.

methods.name: _add_ref
methods.docstring: Add a variable reference.

methods.name: visit_FunctionDef
methods.docstring: Handle function definition - parameters are definitions.

methods.name: visit_AsyncFunctionDef
methods.docstring: Handle async function - same as regular function.

methods.name: visit_Assign
methods.docstring: Handle assignment: target = value.

methods.name: visit_AnnAssign
methods.docstring: Handle annotated assignment: target: type = value.

methods.name: visit_AugAssign
methods.docstring: Handle augmented assignment: target += value.

methods.name: _visit_target
methods.docstring: Visit an assignment target.

methods.name: visit_For
methods.docstring: Handle for loop - target is definition.

methods.name: visit_With
methods.docstring: Handle with statement - optional_vars are definitions.

methods.name: visit_ExceptHandler
methods.docstring: Handle except - name is definition.

methods.name: visit_Name
methods.docstring: Handle name reference - context determines if it's a use.

methods.name: visit_comprehension
methods.docstring: Handle comprehension - target is definition, iter/ifs are uses.

methods.name: visit_ListComp
methods.docstring: Handle list comprehension.

methods.name: visit_SetComp
methods.docstring: Handle set comprehension.

methods.name: visit_DictComp
methods.docstring: Handle dict comprehension.

methods.name: visit_GeneratorExp
methods.docstring: Handle generator expression.

methods.name: visit_Lambda
methods.docstring: Handle lambda - parameters are definitions.

methods.name: generic_visit
methods.docstring: Visit all child nodes.

classes.name: PythonReachingDefsAnalyzer
classes.docstring:
Reaching definitions analysis for Python code.

Uses worklist algorithm on basic blocks to compute
which definitions reach each use.

methods.name: compute_def_use_chains
methods.docstring:
Compute def-use chains using reaching definitions.

Simplified algorithm:
1. Process lines in order
2. Track which definition of each var is "active"
3. When we see a use, link to active definition
4. When we see a def, it becomes the active definition

classes.name: CFGReachingDefsAnalyzer
classes.docstring:
CFG-based reaching definitions analysis.

Uses worklist algorithm on CFG blocks to properly handle branches.
Definitions from both if/else branches reach uses after merge point.

methods.name: _get_block_lines
methods.docstring: Get line range covered by a block.

methods.name: compute_def_use_chains
methods.docstring:
Compute def-use chains using CFG-based reaching definitions.

Algorithm:
1. Initialize reaching_in[block] = {} for all blocks
2. For entry block, reaching_in = parameter defs
3. Iterate until fixed point:
   - reaching_in[block] = merge(reaching_out[pred] for pred in predecessors)
   - reaching_out[block] = gen[block] ∪ (reaching_in[block] - kill[block])
4. Build edges from reaching defs to uses

classes.name: TreeSitterDefUseVisitor
classes.docstring:
Extract variable definitions and uses from tree-sitter parse tree.

Works for TypeScript/JavaScript, Go, and Rust.

methods.docstring: Get source text for a node.

methods.docstring: Visit a node and its children.

methods.docstring: Process a node based on its type.

classes.name: LuaDefUseVisitor
classes.docstring:
Extract variable definitions and uses from Lua tree-sitter parse tree.

Lua variables:
- local x = value (local declaration)
- x = value (assignment, may create global)
- function parameters
- for loop variables

classes.name: ElixirDefUseVisitor
classes.docstring:
Extract variable definitions and uses from Elixir tree-sitter parse tree.

Elixir uses pattern matching for variable binding:
- x = value (assignment/match)
- {a, b} = tuple (destructuring)
- def func(x, y) (parameters)

functions.name: extract_python_dfg
functions.docstring:
Extract DFG for a Python function using CFG-based reaching definitions.

This properly handles control flow - definitions from both if/else
branches will reach uses after the merge point.

Args:
    code: Python source code
    function_name: Name of function to analyze

Returns:
    DFGInfo with variable references and def-use chains

functions.name: extract_python_dfg_with_cfg
functions.docstring:
Extract DFG for a Python function using CFG-based reaching definitions.

This properly handles control flow - definitions from both if/else
branches will reach uses after the merge point.

Args:

    function_name: Name of function to analyze

Returns:
    DFGInfo with variable references and def-use chains

functions.name: _find_lua_function_by_name
functions.docstring:
Find a Lua function node by name in tree-sitter tree.

Handles both:
- function name() ... end (function_declaration)
- local function name() ... end (function_declaration with local)

functions.name: _find_elixir_function_by_name
functions.docstring:
Find an Elixir function node by name in tree-sitter tree.

Elixir functions are defined with def/defp macros:
- def function_name(args) do ... end
- defp private_function(args) do ... end

functions.name: extract_elixir_dfg
functions.docstring:
Extract DFG for an Elixir function.

Args:
    code: Elixir source code
    function_name: Name of function to analyze

Returns:
    DFGInfo with variable references and def-use chains
